title: Worship of three types | BG 17.04 - 17.06 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Topic is three kinds of worship. In India we specifically notice that there are
many many types of temples, people worshipping different gods, different
deities, different devatas, different festivals being observed. Every state
have their own festival, religious festivals. So in today's session we will
be discussing some of the fundamental concepts with respect to why so many
gods, why so many religions. Although we have covered at different times but
nevertheless it's very important for us to have
a very good understanding of this subject. Dedicated to Srila Prabhupada, our
founder and beloved spiritual master. Text 4th from the 17th chapter. Men in
the mode of goodness worship the demigods. Who are the demigods? Devatas.
Actually in India we have a concept that there are many many
gods and you can worship any one of them. Basically you need something to
worship. You need something as an object of your faith and whatever resonates
with you, whatever you're comfortable with, you can worship and it's all one
and the same. So in the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna very clearly
demarcates or distinguishes between the Devatas and the supreme personality of
Godhead, Bhagwan. Who are the Devi and Devatas? Devi and
Devatas are empowered representatives of the Lord. Just like we all are Indians
and
Some Indians by following a democratic process become cabinet ministers,
some become state ministers, some become chief ministers, some become prime ministers.
So they are Indians only like us, potentially we all can become prime minister of the country,
we can become chief minister of the country, more so in present situation, deadlock.
So different Devi and Devatas are devotees of the Lord who have been empowered by the Lord
to take care of different administrative affairs in this universe. Like we have Sun God,
we have Varuna, we have Chandra, we have all different Devi and Devatas.
So they are also living entities.
They are also Jeevatattva and worship of the Devatas is not equivalent to worship of
the Supreme Lord. Devatas cannot give us liberation from this material existence.
They can give us material benedictions. They can give us some,
we have some desires to be fulfilled by worshipping different Devatas, they can be fulfilled.
Material desires can be fulfilled because they are very materially empowered.
Just like chief minister, if you are very close to him, if you want to, he can give you lot of
blessings, lot of your work can happen because he is in an empowered position.
So men in mode of goodness worship the Devatas. Those in the mode of passion worship the demons.
And that's how you see different kinds of worship.
Sometimes have you seen people kind of worship which is very very, people pierce through their
tongue or through their face. How many of you have seen that? At least photographs, if not live.
And then covered, smeared face with different kind and you know especially in Kumbh Mela,
you see all kinds of Babas with all kinds of Vibhuthis, all kinds of ash, matted hairs.
And those in the mode of ignorance worship ghosts and spirits. In India, there are places people go
to remote villages and there are some trees which are said to be places where the ghosts are living
and they worship them. Different kinds of black magic, this tantric kind of worship is practiced.
So all kinds of situations are. So what motivates different people, it's the modes influencing them.
If a person is predominantly in Satva Guna, he will be worshipping of one kind. His worship will
be of one kind. If he is in Raja Guna, his worship will be of one type. In Tama Guna, another type.
Is Satva Guna worship the highest?
So Krishna in the Gita talks about three modes. Satva Guna, Raja Guna and Tama Guna. And over and above, these three modes are the highest.
One of the highest modes is Shuddha Satva. That's why he says Traigunya Vishaya Veda, Nishtraigunya Bhavarjuna.
You rise above the flowery language of the Vedas.
Even mode of goodness is better than passion, better than ignorance, but it's not the highest.
It's a springboard by which we can jump to or move to transcendence. Transcendence means you transcend the mode.
You cross over.
Even Satva Guna is contaminating.
So those who are in goodness, they will worship Devatas.
They'll be very religious. They'll observe various ceremonies. They will get up in the morning.
They'll do their pujas, but materially motivated. Predominant thing which is driving them is Sukh Shanti.
Prosperity.
And materially well situation. Which is not the ultimate goal of religion.
What is the ultimate goal of religion? Anyone?
Ultimate goal of religion is to come out of this material existence, cycle of birth and death,
and go back to our original home, Kingdom of God. That's the goal.
So now, we see people worshipping different Gods and visiting various places of worship.
Yesterday, you know, the entire nation was engulfed with the news of Ayodhya verdict.
And we have different temples, different Devi and Devatas being worshipped.
Now, question might come in the mind of a person who is an agnostic.
Who is an agnostic?
Who is an agnostic?
Why so many Gods? Why so many Gods? Okay, either it could be because people are confused,
no clear idea, so everyone is worshipping their own thing. Doesn't make sense. Why so
many Gods? So many different kinds of worship. So is that kind of a, there is no concreteness,
there is no clear understanding, everyone is coming up with their own understanding
of God, own kind of worship. What is it? So why so many Gods? And a second fundamental
question which can arise in the mind of a person, why so many religions? And that too,
religions which contradict, the instructions of one religion might contradict with one.
One religion says, okay eat meat. And they have some festivals which advocate killing
of animals, like we have Bakri, that kind of thing.
And there is another religion which says, don't eat meat. And in the name of religion
people are fighting and killing each other. So person who is sceptic will say, kyaha hai?
What is this confusion? Why so many Gods? Why so many religions? In the name of religion
people killing each other and fighting and having all sorts of you know, unmentionable
things happening.
the name of religion. What is all this? How many of us say that today world is in a chaos,
in a mess because of religion? Raise your hands again please, little higher. How many
of us say it's not because of religion? 4, 5, 6, 7. Why it's not because of religion?
We see people are fighting in the name of religion. Yesterday, 4000 paramilitary forces
were rushed to the UP because they knew in the name of religion people will.
Fight. Why police is required? Because people are going to fight, people are going to kill
each other, there are going to be riots, there are going to be communal tensions in the name

will not build it, this is Babri Masjid. Who is the other one who said that it's not because
of religion people are fighting? Anybody here? Yes, Rameshji.
Babri Masjid.
Babri Masjid.
Yes, it is not because of religion, it is because of fanatical understanding of religion.
Fanatical, you all understand? No proper understanding of religion. Half baked, sentimental
understanding of religion. I will come to that, what Prabhupada says and what is the
true understanding of religion. So, another few questions which can be very perplexing,
can be very bewildering is whom do I follow? There are so many types of God, so many types
of worship. Whom do I follow? Whom do I believe? If you go to Christians, they will say Jesus
is the only way. You come here and say Krishna. Yes or no? Everyone is a fanatic. Everyone

And they quote too. In Bhagavad Gita it is said this, in Bible it is said that. Whom do
I follow? Whom do I believe? And which God should I worship? And people take the easy
route, they keep everything.
That's why typical altar in an Indian house, you will find some 20-30. How many of us agree

Puttaparthi Sai Baba, someone has given Shiva Linga, they will also keep it. Yes or no?
So then the whole thing is, no one should get angry. So we take the easy route. This is also
one of the reasons, I mean, okay you say, we don't have clear understanding, we keep
everything. Those who are supreme in this will also be fine. Why we have to ascertain?
We keep everything.
Yes.
Yes.
Yes.
Everyone claims that his path or his God to be the best. This is the problem and that's

Christians are trying to convert others. They also want to make others Christian. They
have their targets, they have their goals. My God is better than your God. And in the

Now, the true understanding is just like we have one son, but called by different names
in different languages, in different countries, likewise, there is only one God.
The definition of God is the Adi Purusha, the original person, the cause of all causes.
When there was nothing, he was there.
Sarva Karana Karanam.
So that being the understanding of God, the cause of all causes, the supreme person, the
supreme being, the source of everything, the source of creation, the understanding is there
is one and only one God.
If that is being the so, the same God is addressed differently in different religions by name
Allah, Father in heaven, Krishna, the supreme personality of Godhead, just like you have
Surya, Ravi, Bhaskara and the same son in different languages, in different countries,
in different religions being called by different names.
All bona fide religions and scriptures.
Being word of that one God teach the same truth in essence.
The ultimate objective of all religions is the same.
The essence is the same.
The principles are the same.
What they are meant for is the same.
The objective is the same.
The purpose for which it is being propagated is the same.
What is that purpose?
What is the purpose of all religions?
Irrespective of the details, details may vary from one religion to another.
What is the purpose of all religions?
What is the purpose of all religions?
Bring us closer to God.
Connect us with God.

I am also forgetful of God, you are also forgetful of God, there are many others who are forgetful

So the essence, what is the underlying essence of all religions?
God is one, develop love for God, obey God's laws.

Every religion has that.

Return to the kingdom of God.
So that ultimately we come out of this cycle of birth and death, this material existence

That's the objective.

There's an objective, there's a purpose to spread the message of Gita.

He gives his message.
He gives his message.
He gives his message.

This brings the difference.
God is one, the message is one, essential principles are the same.
But why so many gods?
Why so many religions?
Why so many forms of worship?

The same message is given by God and his representatives because the audience are in different time,

In elementary school, a child is taught very basic addition, subtraction, multiplication.
So, Prabhupada gives a very good example.
In elementary school, a child is taught very basic addition, subtraction, multiplication.
2 plus 2 is 4.
2 plus 3 is 5.
3 minus 2 is 1.
Now, if the child asks the teacher while being taught some subtraction, 3 minus 2 is 1.

What is 2 minus 5?
The child asks.
What will the teacher say?
Teacher will say, it is not possible.
In elementary school, teacher who has done B.Ed, who is quite well versed, knowing that

understanding, teacher says it's not possible.
And the teacher will tell also, if you have 5 apples, how can you give 8 apples to someone?
So it is not possible.
The same teacher in high school will say it is minus 3.

So all levels are being taught Maths by a Maths teacher.
But
The

Now I am going to tell you, I shall declare unto you this in full, this knowledge, this

In other words, this is no way to say that.

Because it completes.

For we can see you again and again.
For we can see you again and again.

Kunti Maharani, knowing that ultimate goal is to get freed from the cycle of birth and death, for which we have to remember the Lord.
He says better let the miseries be there because miseries are an impetus for us to remember you.
When we have good time, everything is comfortable, we are in the kingdom, now there is no troubles, no demons attacking us, we will forget you.
What is the use of that kind of prosperity? So better let there be more and more calamities so that we can remember you.

O Jagadisha, kaam aye means I do not desire, that's not my objective, that's not what I am worshipping you for.
And then what I want is your causeless mercy of devotional service.
In my life, birth after birth.
It doesn't matter where I take birth.
All I want is opportunity to serve you, opportunity to remember you.
See how these instructions are different from typical religious understanding you have in the society.
Typical religious this thing is, I want money, I want wealth, I want name, I want fame, I want followers.

Dharma means code of conduct based on the principles of religion based on instructions of God.
Animals don't have that ability to inquire, to understand.
Athato brahma jignasa.
Now that you have got human birth, inquire into the self.
Who is God?
Who am I?
Where do I come from?
What happens after death?
Animals can't understand this.
So solution is to practice two religious principles and not do away with religion.
Lot of people have done that.
So called educated people, they say I am a spiritual person but I am not a religious person.
What they basically mean to say is I am honest.
I am truthful.
I don't hurt anyone.
I am a good human being.
But I may not believe in God.
Done away with religion.
Highest dharma is karma yoga.
Be a karma yogi.
Do your work.
Work is worship.
Be a good human being.
That's what.
What is the use of chanting, going to temple, remembering God, thinking of God.
After all it has resulted in all this chaos.
Do away with it.
Many so called intelligent educated people have taken to this path.
I don't go to temples.
I don't worship God.
I am a good human.
My goal is to be a good human being.
So during the Second World War, many...
If it is not based on proper understanding,
when the war was happening, many wives of the soldiers who were in the battlefront,
they actually went to the church, prayed, prayed and prayed, days together.
And many of them lost their husband in the war.
And their faith was completely shattered.
So religious...
Lot of people say, I am worshipping God but why so many problems are coming in my life?
Because we have a sentimental understanding of religion.
We have an own understanding.
I am worshipping means no problem should come in my life.
Or problem should go away.
Then only the path which I am following is the right path.
Which is not true.
Think that...
Now there are many people who subscribe to the understanding that any God can be worshipped
and the result is the same.
Anything which resonates with you, which is also...
Krishna also in the Gita says,
If you worship a particular God, you go to that particular God.
There are different Devi, Devatas.
They have heavenly planets.
Just like you have Indralok, Chandralok and all that thing.
So it is not that you worship anyone and everyone and it is the same thing.
They think that actual truth is impersonal Brahman
and they dispose of all worshipable objects at the ultimate end.
Now some of them also subscribe to the understanding that
we may worship some Devi, Devatas
like we have deities in our puja room and all that.
These are all meant to focus our mind.
Ultimately, God is impersonal.
Impersonal means He doesn't have form.
He is not a person.
So these are all just for...
Just like you have to climb up.
That's the destination.
You may use staircase.
It's just a means to an end.
After you reach the top, drop the staircase.
You are hungry.
You want to eat something.
You may require plate, glass, cups, all that, spoons.
Once you have eaten, dispose off.
It's not required.
So that is very blasphemous.
Because they are saying ultimately,
what we are worshipping, all that, is just a means to an end.
It's not an end.
Many worship...
It's dictated...
It's dictated by mode of ignorance.
Worship dead spirits.
A lot of people, have you heard of?
They call the spirit.
And the coins and this thing.
All this kind of things.
So, different forms of worship.
Srimad Bhagavatam.
It's said, Satvam Vishuddham Vasudeva Satvitam.
Srimad Bhagavatam advises the worship of Vasudevam.
Which is in the form of Vishuddha Satvam.
Shuddha Satva.
It's above the mode of goodness.
Text 5 & 6.
Those who undergo severe austerities and penances not recommended in scriptures,
performing them out of pride,
egoism, lust and attachment,
who are impelled by passion
and who torture their bodily organs
as well as super soul dwelling within,
are known as demons.
So, a lot of people who are doing lot of austerities
in the name of religion,
in the name of God,
torturing themselves,
fasting for days together,
months together,
years together.
The people like that
may not be authorized.
The people who manufacture the modes of austerities and penances
and they take pride in saying,
I fasted for one month.
The amount of pain which endures by fasting,
much more pleasure one gets by
by getting that
pride or that satisfaction that I fasted.
The scriptures recommended fasting for spiritual advancement,
not for some political or social.
A lot of people end up fasting for political and social purposes.
To get their demands pressed.
By such activities,
unfortunately,
in the text Krishna is mentioning,
pain comes to them and also
to the super soul,
the Lord who is situated in the body.
We unnecessarily pain the Lord
by following something which is unauthorized.
If one is fortunate enough
to be guided by a genuine spiritual master,
he will tread the right path
with the right understanding.
He will be blessed.
So that way,
one has to be really fortunate
to get a bona fide spiritual master
who can enlighten one with the right understanding.
